description_long: This object is a group of three distinct chairs, each with a unique design and construction. The leftmost chair features a wooden frame with a ladder-back design and a square seat, likely made of wood or a wood composite. The center chair has a rounded backrest with vertical slats and a circular seat, constructed from metal with a cushioned or wooden seat. The rightmost chair is a simple, utilitarian design with a metal frame and a rectangular seat and backrest, both of which appear to be covered in a worn, light-colored material. All three chairs show signs of wear, suggesting they have been used in public or semi-public urban environments. The materials range from wood to metal, with some fabric or padding visible on the seats. These chairs are typical of those found in parks, waiting areas, or outdoor cafes.
description: A group of three worn urban chairs: one wooden ladder-back, one round metal-back, and one simple metal-framed chair with a light seat.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three chairs' seat and backrest designs: wooden ladder-back, rounded metal-back, and plain metal-framed.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and thickness of each chair, with the round-backed chair's profile most prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backrest structures, especially the ladder-back and slatted metal designs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the backs and seat thicknesses, with the wooden chair's backrest most visible.
